stories.title: Column | How to stop ChatGPT from ruining how you think
stories.simple_summary: An opinion piece warns that using ChatGPT too much might change how you think and reason about problems.
stories.why_it_matters: If you rely on AI to do all your thinking, you might lose your own problem-solving skills — like how a calculator can make you forget how to do math in your head.

stories.title: Cybersecurity expert warns China's AI advances could intensify global tech competition
stories.simple_summary: China is getting better at AI, which is making other countries worried about competition.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to politicians and large companies — it won't directly change your daily life or small business soon.

stories.title: AI startup CEO pleaded guilty in US to trading on insider tips from lawyers
stories.simple_summary: A boss of an AI company got caught using secret tips from lawyers to make unfair trades and make money illegally.
stories.why_it_matters: This is a legal case about one person breaking the rules — it doesn't affect ordinary people or small businesses.

stories.title: UCSD aims to put AI research into practice
stories.simple_summary: A university is trying to take AI research discoveries and turn them into real tools that hospitals and doctors can actually use.
stories.why_it_matters: Over time, this could mean better medical care, though it will take years before you'd notice any difference.

stories.title: Solos debuts an even lighter version of its camera-less smart glasses
stories.simple_summary: A company made new smart glasses that are half the weight of last year's version and use voice commands instead of cameras.
stories.why_it_matters: If you're interested in wearable tech that you talk to, this is getting lighter and more practical — though these are still expensive gadgets for early adopters.

stories.title: Stymied datacentre projects threaten global AI revolution
stories.simple_summary: There aren't enough large computer warehouses being built fast enough to support AI growth around the world.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to tech investors and AI companies — nothing you need to worry about as a small-business owner right now.

stories.title: Amazon Returns to US Bond Market to Fund AI Infrastructure Build
stories.simple_summary: Amazon borrowed billions of dollars from investors to pay for building more powerful computer systems for AI.
stories.why_it_matters: This is a financial move by a huge company — it doesn't directly affect small businesses or everyday people.

stories.title: Savi’s app aims to protect consumers from realistic AI scams like kidnappers demanding ransom
stories.simple_summary: A new app is being launched to help warn people when someone is trying to scam them with fake AI voices, like pretending to be a kidnapper demanding money.
stories.why_it_matters: If you or your family get calls from scammers using AI voices, this app might help protect you from falling for it.

stories.title: The first American autonomous ground vehicles are fighting in Ukraine
stories.simple_summary: The U.S. military is using over 100 self-driving robot vehicles (like small ATVs) in Ukraine to help with the war.
stories.why_it_matters: This is a military application in a war zone — it doesn't affect your life or small business.

stories.title: The ‘first’ AI-run ransomware attack still needed a human
stories.simple_summary: A computer attack using AI still needed a human to choose the target and set up most of the work — the AI just did the final technical step.
stories.why_it_matters: This means AI isn't taking over criminal attacks on its own yet; criminals still need to do the thinking — nothing new to fear as a business owner.

stories.title: US investors will soon get access to SK Hynix, another memory maker riding the AI boom
stories.simple_summary: A Korean computer chip company is selling shares to American investors because AI is driving huge demand for their products.
stories.why_it_matters: This is a stock market event that matters to investors — nothing you need to act on.

stories.title: Vercel CEO Guillermo Rauch on the fight to split off models from agents
stories.simple_summary: A tech company leader is talking about separating AI models from AI agents to make them work better and cheaper in real-world use.
stories.why_it_matters: This is a technical discussion for engineers and tech companies — not something that affects ordinary people or small businesses.
